notes.345ec2cb736b619c26b5e443dc6c96d9_x0: F is the standout—'fragile authority in the camp's cruel hierarchy' captures the structural dynamics of the Blockältester role perfectly. The other candidates treat this as a biographical detail rather than a systemic feature. E usefully references page numbers but doesn't analyse the power structure.
notes.345ec2cb736b619c26b5e443dc6c96d9_x1: A and D both touch on the camp infrastructure (Kapos, SS officials), which is crucial context. The camp system (Buchenwald-Gusen-Mauthausen network) is under-explored by most candidates. F's speculation about perpetrator status is inappropriate but at least acknowledges the complexity of camp hierarchies.
notes.345ec2cb736b619c26b5e443dc6c96d9_x2: E and F best capture the systematic nature of the persecution—the deliberate starvation rations, the engineered 'escape' shootings. The first-person accounts are moving but miss the institutional analysis. The cowshed detail is important as it shows improvised infrastructure.
notes.345ec2cb736b619c26b5e443dc6c96d9_x3: All candidates handle the power dynamics reasonably well. The key insight—that anti-fascist prisoners' resistance led to purging criminal overseers—reflects a shift in internal camp power structures. B and D capture this best. The Kommandant's role as institutional actor is well-handled by most.
notes.345ec2cb736b619c26b5e443dc6c96d9_x4: B and C best capture the institutional dimension—the SS's deliberate creation of a pariah class and the systematic nature of the violence. D's 'state-sanctioned violence' framing is excellent. A is competent but doesn't push the analysis far enough.
